title: Living in Boulder 3 - 30 - 2017: 640 Kilometers in background

enrichment.summary: Kyle logs his day on March 30, 2017 late at night. He lost several hours of work when his computer crashed, wiping tracing work that involves 1600 different tracings. He also discusses his ongoing study of libertarianism, his response to their 'people are lazy' argument via a 'by schools' (bicycle?) daily routine concept, and his research into dark money by reading Jane Mayer's book 'Dark Money' while pausing to Wikipedia foundations and institutes she mentions.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Has a counter — a daily routine (biking/by schools) is self-fixing and makes laziness a non-issue
Studying it to understand how to redirect finances; implies opposition to dark money influence
Actively leaving comments on libertarian content and saving them for his own website

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle got up late because he was working until one or two in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle spent the day tracing stuff and lost four hours of work when his computer crashed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle lost 1600 different tracings when his computer crashed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is worried about how long it will take to recreate the lost tracings on a grid.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle doubts he can complete the project on his current hardware.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is studying libertarianism and commenting on libertarian content.

claims.subject: libertarians
claims.claim: Libertarians believe people are lazy and shouldn't be given anything because they will remain lazy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle argues that his daily routine of talking for two hours proves he is not lazy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes his routine is self-fixing and fun, so everyone should do it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks his routine is cheap and there's no reason not to do it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is copying libertarian content to use later for his own website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle struggles with typing on his computer and wants to experiment with dictation.

claims.subject: backgrounds
claims.claim: The backgrounds Kyle is working on are 640 kilometers in diameter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hasn't memorized the details of his website yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is listening to the book 'Dark Money' by Jane Mayer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle pauses the book to research foundations, institutes, or people mentioned on Wikipedia or YouTube.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes understanding dark money is necessary to direct finances effectively.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hopes it won't take too long to implement his ideas.
